puzzle.title: The Herald of an Unprecedented Sin
puzzle.intro: Some stories in the Quran reveal the depth of corruption an entire society can fall into. This messenger stood alone, pleading with a people drowned in a perversion that no nation before them had ever pursued. Despite years of warning, hearts remained hardened, and the divine decree arrived in a most terrifying manner. Can you identify this noble messenger of Allah?
clues: I was sent as a warner to a people consumed by a perversion that no nation before them had ever committed.
My people called me a liar and threatened to expel my followers and me from the land.
Honored visitors came to my home in the form of striking young men, and I felt deep distress for their safety.
My people demanded I surrender my guests to them, unaware that these visitors were angels sent by Allah.
My own spouse did not share in my faith and chose to remain behind with the doomed.
I was saved with my daughters at dawn, as a devastating rain of stones fell and our cities were overturned.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Lut
Prophet Shu'aib
Prophet Hud
Prophet Nuh
Prophet Salih

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: A great flood engulfing the earth
Loss of eyesight from long suffering
A tyrant claiming divinity
Being thrown into a blazing fire
Being demanded to surrender honored guests
trial.answer: Being demanded to surrender honored guests

location.label: Key Element
options: A great ark built by hand
A staff that split the sea
A miraculous she-camel
A blazing fire
Overturned cities

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Drowned while pursuing the believers
Saved from a furnace of fire unharmed
Lifted up to the heavens alive
Family perished in a great flood
Family saved except for an unfaithful wife
outcome.answer: Family saved except for an unfaithful wife

puzzle.arabic: فَمَا وَجَدْنَا فِيهَا غَيْرَ بَيْتٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ
puzzle.verse: And We found not within them other than a [single] house of Muslims. (51:36)
